sections.headline_html:
Search visibility.
What’s working and what’s not.
sections.lead: I ran a technical audit on summerhill.build and crawled 30 pages. The site has a strong foundation with good meta data and fast response times, but there’s one critical gap and a major image SEO issue holding it back.

findings.title: Valid SSL Certificate
findings.detail: Site is served over HTTPS with no mixed content issues.

findings.title: Strong Meta Title
findings.detail: "Inspired Building - Summerhill Fine Homes" (41 characters). Clear, branded, good length.

findings.title: Meta Description Present
findings.detail: "Custom home builder specializing in sustainability and performance" (137 chars). Solid description that communicates the value proposition.

findings.title: Single H1 Tag
findings.detail: "Inspired Building" — clean hierarchy, no duplicate H1s.

findings.title: Open Graph Tags
findings.detail: og:title and og:image present. Social sharing will display correctly.

findings.title: Fast Server Response
findings.detail: 78ms to first byte. Well-optimized hosting.

findings.title: Sitemap Exists
findings.detail: sitemap_index.xml found with 8 URLs indexed. Google can discover your pages.

findings.title: Canonical Tags
findings.detail: Proper canonical URLs set. No duplicate content issues.

findings.title: No Structured Data (Schema.org)
findings.detail: Zero JSON-LD markup found on the site. This is the single biggest SEO gap. Adding LocalBusiness, Organization, and Project schema would help Google understand what Summerhill does, where you operate, and what you’ve built. This directly impacts local search visibility and rich snippets.

findings.title: 1,435 Images Missing Alt Text
findings.detail: Of 1,637 total images across the site, 87.6% have no alt text. The homepage alone has 35 of 50 images (70%) without alt tags. This is a significant accessibility and image SEO issue. Every image is a missed opportunity to rank in Google Image search for terms like “custom home Sunshine Coast.”

findings.title: Only 8 URLs in Sitemap
findings.detail: 30 crawlable pages but only 8 in the sitemap. The remaining 22 pages may not be getting indexed efficiently. Expanding the sitemap to include all project pages would improve discoverability.

insight.title: SEO Bottom Line
insight.content_html: The site’s technical foundation is solid — fast, secure, well-structured meta data. The two critical fixes are structured data (schema markup for LocalBusiness + Projects) and image alt text across 1,435 images. These are both one-time fixes that permanently improve search visibility.

sections.lead: Local SEO determines whether Summerhill shows up when someone searches “custom builder near me” on the Sunshine Coast. This is where the most significant gaps exist.

findings.title: Google Business Profile Is Active
findings.detail: Summerhill has a live, active Google Business Profile. This is the foundation for local search visibility and Google Maps presence. Ahead of most Sunshine Coast builders on this front.

findings.title: GBP Optimization Opportunity
findings.detail: Verify that the profile has complete service areas, business categories, photos, and regular posts. An optimized GBP with 10+ reviews significantly outranks a basic listing.

findings.title: No LocalBusiness Schema on Website
findings.detail: No structured data connecting your website to your GBP. Adding LocalBusiness JSON-LD schema reinforces your local presence and helps Google connect the two.

findings.title: Listed on Houzz
findings.detail: Summerhill has a Houzz profile. This is a strong authoritative backlink for residential builders.

findings.title: Not Found on HomeStars
findings.detail: No HomeStars profile detected. HomeStars ranks well for “builder” searches in BC and provides a trust signal plus backlink.

findings.title: Not Found on CHBA Directory
findings.detail: No CHBA BC directory listing detected. This is an authoritative industry backlink that signals credibility to Google. Also required for Georgie Award eligibility.

findings.title: NAP Consistency Unverified
findings.detail: Your business name, address, and phone number (NAP) should be identical across your website footer, Google Business Profile, Houzz, and all other listings. Inconsistencies confuse Google and hurt local rankings.

insight.title: Local SEO Bottom Line
insight.content_html: The GBP is a strong start. The next steps are optimizing it with regular photo uploads, requesting Google reviews from past clients (target 10+), and adding LocalBusiness schema to the website so Google connects your GBP to your site. The directory gaps (HomeStars, CHBA) are secondary but worth filling for citation building.

sections.headline_html:
How you compare.
On the Sunshine Coast.
sections.lead: Here’s how Summerhill’s online presence stacks up against the builders you’re actually competing against for Sunshine Coast clients.

insight.title: The Local Competitive Gap
insight.content_html:
On the Sunshine Coast, Lincoln Construction is the one to watch — they have award finalist badges, a downloadable planning guide, and a strong content strategy. Jenkins has excellent photography. Everyone else is roughly where Summerhill is or behind.

Summerhill’s site quality is at or above every competitor. The differentiators that would pull ahead: schema markup, Google Business Profile, awards submissions, and video content. None of these competitors have video. First mover wins.

sections.headline_html:
What you have.
What you could do with it.
sections.lead: An audit of Summerhill’s current photography, content, and visual asset coverage across the site.

findings.title: 30 Crawlable Pages
findings.detail: Solid page count for a custom builder. The site has real depth.

findings.title: 20 Project Pages with Descriptions
findings.detail: Every project page has 100+ words of descriptive content. This is significantly better than most builders who have image-only portfolios.

findings.title: Good Homepage Content (473 Words)
findings.detail: Homepage has strong copy that communicates Summerhill’s value proposition. Well above the 300-word minimum for SEO.

findings.title: Strong Visual Coverage
findings.detail: Project pages average 70.7 images each. 1,637 total images across the site. This is an excellent visual library.

findings.title: Blog Has Only 1 Post
findings.detail: A blog section exists but has minimal content. Monthly posts targeting “custom home building Sunshine Coast,” “passive house BC,” or “CLT construction” would compound organic traffic over time.

findings.title: 1,435 Images Without Alt Text
findings.detail: This is the single biggest content gap. Every image without alt text is invisible to Google Image search. For an architectural builder, image search is a major discovery channel that’s currently being left on the table.

findings.title: No Video Content on Site
findings.detail: Zero project films, walkthrough videos, or social reels embedded on the site. Video increases time-on-page (an SEO signal) and is the highest-engagement format for social media.

findings.title: Build Process Not Documented
findings.detail: Summerhill’s CLT, passive house, and mass timber construction methods are visually compelling. Documenting the build process creates content that performs well on social, builds trust with prospects, and differentiates from competitors who only show finished homes.

insight.title: Content Bottom Line
insight.content_html: The site’s content foundation is stronger than most custom builders. 20 project pages with real descriptions is rare in this market. The wins are specific: fix 1,435 missing alt tags (one-time project), start a monthly blog, and add video and build documentation to turn a good portfolio into a content engine that drives organic traffic.

sections.lead: Three moves that would have the biggest impact on Summerhill’s visibility, based on what the audit actually found.

steps.title: Schema + Alt Text
steps.description: Add LocalBusiness JSON-LD schema to the site. Fix 1,435 missing alt tags with descriptive, keyword-rich text. These are one-time projects that permanently improve search visibility.

steps.title: Video + Build Documentation
steps.description: Start capturing CLT, passive house, and mass timber construction professionally. Create monthly social reels and a year-end project film. First mover on the Sunshine Coast for video content.

steps.title: Awards Pipeline
steps.description: Submit 2-3 strongest projects to Georgie Awards and CHBA National. Every shoot photographed to award spec going forward. Lincoln already has finalist badges. Summerhill’s work is stronger.

insight.title: This Is Where a Creative Partner Comes In
insight.content_html:
The site’s foundation is strong. What’s missing is the ongoing content engine: professional project photography, build documentation, video, social content, and the SEO layer that makes all of it discoverable. Every gap in this audit is something I handle as part of the Creative Partner retainer.

See the full Creative Partner proposal for pricing, tier options, and a 3-month pilot roadmap.
